title: MySQL CPU 100%: OS thread to SQL runbook
scenario: mysqld CPU saturates and an agent must identify the SQL/session responsible
symptoms: mysql process CPU exceeds 100% in top
business reports slow or stuck requests
preconditions: Linux shell access to the database host
permission to query information_schema.processlist and performance_schema.threads
performance_schema enabled for thread and statement inspection
immediate_mitigation: confirm CPU and memory pressure before killing sessions
identify exact processlist_id and confirm business owner before kill

diagnostic_steps.purpose: confirm mysqld is the CPU consumer
diagnostic_steps.expected_signal: mysqld CPU is abnormal while memory remains acceptable

diagnostic_steps.purpose: find hot OS thread under mysqld
diagnostic_steps.expected_signal: one THREAD_OS_ID has abnormal CPU, example 2775

diagnostic_steps.purpose: map OS thread to MySQL connection and internal thread
commands: select a.user, a.host, a.db, b.thread_os_id, b.thread_id, a.id processlist_id from information_schema.processlist a, performance_schema.threads b where a.id = b.processlist_id and b.thread_os_id = 2775;
diagnostic_steps.expected_signal: returns user/host/db/thread_id/processlist_id for the hot OS thread

diagnostic_steps.purpose: read current SQL for the internal thread
commands: select * from performance_schema.events_statements_current where thread_id in (select thread_id from performance_schema.threads where thread_os_id = 2775);
diagnostic_steps.expected_signal: returns current statement for the hot thread

decision_branches.condition: current SQL is confirmed harmful and business owner approves interruption
decision_branches.action: kill the MySQL processlist_id, not the OS thread id
use slow log and explain plan rather than current statement tables

root_causes: CPU-heavy SQL such as order by rand() on large table
fixes: kill confirmed offending session as immediate containment
add appropriate indexes for where/join/order/group fields
review database parameters such as innodb_buffer_pool_size where supported by evidence

risk_operations.caution: Use the MySQL processlist_id returned by the mapping query; killing the wrong session can abort business transactions.
KB mentions parameter optimization but does not provide workload-specific values.

coverage_notes: KB gives command path from OS to MySQL thread and SQL. The kill example in source is ambiguous, so this record adds a caution to use processlist_id.

title: Connection pool exhaustion and possible leak diagnosis (HikariCP partial)
scenario: application times out waiting for database connections or active connections do not return to idle
symptoms: application errors when pool is busy
CPU/memory may remain high if connection creation/destruction is frequent
preconditions: application exposes connection pool metrics or logs
knowledge of HikariCP configuration such as maximumPoolSize, minimumIdle, connectionTimeout
immediate_mitigation: avoid blindly increasing maximumPoolSize without checking database capacity
reduce long-running transactions and restore failed connections

diagnostic_steps.purpose: check pool sizing and wait behavior
diagnostic_steps.expected_signal: too-small pool causes waiting; too-large pool consumes server/database resources

diagnostic_steps.purpose: look for long transactions occupying a connection
commands: review transaction duration in application and database sessions
diagnostic_steps.expected_signal: a transaction holds the same connection for a long time and blocks other requests

commands: periodic SELECT 1 or database ping where supported
diagnostic_steps.expected_signal: invalid connections are removed and replenished

decision_branches.condition: active connections continuously rise and never return
decision_branches.action: suspect leak or long transaction; inspect application code paths that borrow connections

decision_branches.condition: pool is full only during load spikes
decision_branches.action: capacity-test pool size against database capacity; consider queueing, read/write split, or traffic shaping

fixes: ensure connections are closed/returned in finally or framework-managed scope
remove invalid connections with health checks
prevention: run load tests for batch jobs, migrations, and peak traffic

risk_operations.caution: Can move the bottleneck to the database and worsen memory/connection pressure.

coverage_notes: KB has generic connection pool and HikariCP parameter material. It does not provide HikariCP-specific leakDetectionThreshold, ProxyLeakTask, JMX, Micrometer, or stack trace diagnosis. If asked for those specifics, answer: KB 未提供.
